---
title: "05 - GMM Example for Unsupervised State Discovery"
format: pdf
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Objective

Use Gaussian Mixture Models to identify latent operational regimes.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(mclust)
```

## Simulated multivariate sensor features

```{r}
set.seed(314)

n <- 1200
z <- sample(1:3, n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.35, 0.50, 0.15))

dat <- tibble(
  power = rnorm(n, c(22, 56, 78)[z], c(4, 5, 6)[z]),
  vibration = rnorm(n, c(0.9, 2.1, 3.9)[z], c(0.3, 0.4, 0.5)[z]),
  sound = rnorm(n, c(46, 63, 80)[z], c(3, 4, 5)[z]),
  temp = rnorm(n, c(31, 42, 58)[z], c(2, 3, 4)[z])
)
```

## Fit GMM and inspect assignments

```{r}
gmm_fit <- Mclust(dat, G = 1:6)

out <- tibble(
  cluster = factor(gmm_fit$classification),
  uncertainty = gmm_fit$uncertainty
) |>
  mutate(component = as.integer(cluster))

list(
  model = gmm_fit,
  assignments = out,
  bic = gmm_fit$bic
)
